1	---
2	type: task
3	id: OT-0013
4	status: pending
5	priority: high
6	created: 2026-04-01T16:43:00+08:00
7	depends: []
8	blocks: []
9	---
10	
11	# Obsidian-Flavored Markdown Parsing Library
12	
13	## Objective
14	
15	Build `src/libs/obsidian-md/` -- a zero-dependency, roundtrip-safe parser/serializer for the project's Obsidian vault markdown. Three layers: parse/serialize, typed models, AST operations. This is a NEW library -- the existing `src/libs/sections.ts` and `src/tools/obsidian/*.ts` stay untouched. Migration is a separate task.
16	
17	## Architecture
18	
19	```
20	src/libs/obsidian-md/
21	  index.ts          -- re-exports everything
22	  types.ts          -- all interfaces, types, enums
23	  parser.ts         -- parse(markdown) -> Document
24	  serializer.ts     -- serialize(Document) -> markdown
25	  operations.ts     -- section ops, task ops, carry-forward
26	```
27	
28	```mermaid
29	flowchart TD
30	    MD["Markdown string"] --> P["parse()"]
31	    P --> AST["Document AST"]
32	    AST --> OPS["operations.ts"]
33	    OPS --> AST2["Modified AST"]
34	    AST2 --> S["serialize()"]
35	    S --> MD2["Markdown string"]
36	
37	    AST --> FM["frontmatter: Record"]
38	    AST --> SEC["sections: Section[]"]
39	    SEC --> NODES["body: Node[]"]
40	    NODES --> TASK["TaskNode"]
41	    NODES --> TEXT["TextNode"]
42	    NODES --> TABLE["TableNode"]
43	    NODES --> DV["DataviewNode"]
44	```
45	
46	## Constraints
47	
48	- **Zero external dependencies.** No remark, marked, yaml, etc. Use the existing `src/libs/frontmatter.ts` for frontmatter parsing/serialization (it already handles YAML frontmatter, inline arrays, multi-line lists, wikilink quoting).
49	- **Roundtrip-safe.** `serialize(parse(text)) === text` for unchanged documents. Every node stores its `raw` source line(s). Serializer emits `raw` unless the node was mutated (tracked via a `dirty` flag).
50	- **TypeScript, Bun runtime.** No browser compat needed.
51	- **All patterns from actual vault notes must parse correctly.** See "Markdown Patterns" section below.
52	
53	## Markdown Patterns (observed in vault)
54	
55	These are the actual patterns found in daily notes, weekly summaries, and meeting notes. The parser MUST handle all of them.
56	
57	### Frontmatter
58	
59	```yaml
60	---
61	id: daily-20260401
62	title: 2026-04-01
63	tags: [daily]
64	connected:
65	  - "[[notes/daily/2026-03-31]]"
66	---
67	```
68	
69	Reuse `parseFrontmatter()` and `serializeFrontmatter()` from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts`. Do NOT reimplement.
70	
71	### Headings
72	
73	```markdown
74	## Tasks
75	## Log
76	## Session Work
77	### Odelon Miranda
78	```
79	
80	Level 2 (`##`) are section boundaries. Level 3 (`###`) are subsection headers within a section body.
81	
82	### Tasks with bracket IDs
83	
84	```markdown
85	- [ ] [1] Task text
86	- [x] [6] Completed task ✅ 2026-03-31
87	- [/] [3] In-progress task
88	- [>] [1] ~~Deferred text~~ → [[notes/daily/2026-04-01|04-01]]
89	- [-] [10] [log] Cancelled task ❌ 2026-03-31 - reason
90	- [!] [2] Urgent task
91	- [?] [4] Question task
92	```
93	
94	### Sub-tasks (indented, dotted IDs)
95	
96	```markdown
97	- [ ] [5] Parent task
98	  - [ ] [5.1] Sub-task one
99	  - [x] [5.2] Sub-task two
100	  - [ ] [5.3] Sub-task three
101	```
102	
103	Two-space indent. IDs are `{parent}.{child}` format. Sub-tasks can have carry-forward links and doc links just like top-level tasks.
104	
105	### Group labels (bold, non-checkbox)
106	
107	```markdown
108	- **[3] OT-1598: Continue working on auth**
109	- **[4] ~~Moved group~~ → [[notes/daily/2026-03-24|03-24]]**
110	```
111	
112	These are task group headers. They use bold `**`, have bracket IDs, but no checkbox. They can have sub-tasks beneath them.
113	
114	### Doc link references
115	
116	```markdown
117	- [x] [9] Task text (📄 [[notes/docs/prompt-builder-engine|1]] [[notes/docs/prompt-builder-phase1-session2|5]])
118	```
119	
120	The `(📄 ...)` block contains numbered wikilinks. Multiple docs are space-separated inside the parens.
121	
122	### Carry-forward chains
123	
124	```markdown
125	- [ ] [1] Task text ← [[notes/daily/2026-03-24|03-24]] ← [[notes/daily/2026-03-25|03-25]]
126	```
127	
128	Each `← [[path|alias]]` is a history entry showing which daily note the task was carried from. Forward arrows `→ [[path|alias]]` indicate where a deferred task was sent.
129	
130	### Deferred tasks (strikethrough + arrow)
131	
132	```markdown
133	- [>] [1] ~~Original text ← [[...]] ← [[...]]~~ → [[notes/daily/2026-04-01|04-01]]
134	```
135	
136	The entire content is wrapped in `~~strikethrough~~`, followed by `→` and the target link.
137	
138	### Completion markers
139	
140	```markdown
141	- [x] [9] Task text ✅ 2026-03-31
142	- [-] [10] Cancelled task ❌ 2026-03-31 - reason
143	```
144	
145	Completion date after checkmark emoji. Cancellation uses ❌ with optional reason after ` - `.
146	
147	### Dataview inline JS
148	
149	```markdown
150	| Tasks completed | `$= const t = dv.current().file.tasks.where(t => t.section.subpath === "Tasks"); dv.span(t.where(t => t.completed).length + "/" + t.length)` |
151	```
152	
153	These are backtick-wrapped JS expressions starting with `$=`. They appear inside table cells. The parser must preserve them verbatim.
154	
155	### Wikilinks
156	
157	```markdown
158	[[notes/daily/2026-03-31]]
159	[[notes/daily/2026-03-31|03-31]]
160	[[notes/meetings/2026-03-23-skill-demo-mike-sev]]
161	```
162	
163	Path only, or path with `|alias`.
164	
165	### Tables
166	
167	```markdown
168	| Metric | Count |
169	|--------|-------|
170	| Tasks completed | value |
171	```
172	
173	Standard markdown tables. Preserve alignment and cell content exactly.
174	
175	### Regular list items
176	
177	```markdown
178	- 7:54 AM — Dev session: freddie-ai — description
179	- CLI-only for all tool calls — detail
180	- **Test result — description:** detail text
181	```
182	
183	Non-task list items (no checkbox). Used in Log, Decisions, Learned, Notes sections.
184	
185	### Subsection headers within sections
186	
187	```markdown
188	## Session Work
189	### Tested /create-track pipeline end-to-end
190	### Dev session: Phase 1 Suno Style Builder
191	```
192	
193	### Focus areas line
194	
195	```markdown
196	**Focus areas:** (pending)
197	**Focus areas:** Suno vocal production, music cards
198	```
199	
200	Special bold line in Summary section.
201	
202	### Meeting note patterns
203	
204	```markdown
205	## Action Items
206	- [ ] **Odelon** — Submit PR for 60-min token refresh cycle
207	- [ ] **Team** — Investigate locking AirTable view
208	```
209	
210	Bold name prefix on action items.
211	
212	## File-by-File Spec
213	
214	### 1. `types.ts`
215	
216	All types and interfaces. No logic, no imports beyond TypeScript built-ins.
217	
218	```typescript
219	// ── Status ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
220	
221	export type TaskStatus = 'todo' | 'done' | 'in_progress' | 'deferred' | 'cancelled' | 'urgent' | 'question';
222	
223	export const STATUS_CHARS: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
224	  ' ': 'todo',
225	  'x': 'done',
226	  'X': 'done',
227	  '/': 'in_progress',
228	  '>': 'deferred',
229	  '-': 'cancelled',
230	  '!': 'urgent',
231	  '?': 'question',
232	};
233	
234	export const STATUS_TO_CHAR: Record<TaskStatus, string> = {
235	  todo: ' ',
236	  done: 'x',
237	  in_progress: '/',
238	  deferred: '>',
239	  cancelled: '-',
240	  urgent: '!',
241	  question: '?',
242	};
243	
244	// ── Wikilinks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
245	
246	export interface Wikilink {
247	  path: string;        // "notes/daily/2026-03-31"
248	  alias?: string;      // "03-31"
249	}
250	
251	export interface DocLink {
252	  path: string;        // "notes/docs/prompt-builder-engine"
253	  number: number;      // 1
254	}
255	
256	// ── Nodes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
257	
258	export type NodeType = 'task' | 'text' | 'heading' | 'table' | 'blank';
259	
260	export interface BaseNode {
261	  type: NodeType;
262	  line: number;        // 1-based source line number
263	  raw: string;         // original line text (or multi-line for tables)
264	  dirty: boolean;      // true if mutated since parse
265	}
266	
267	export interface Task extends BaseNode {
268	  type: 'task';
269	  id: string;              // "5", "5.3" — from [N] or [N.M] bracket. Empty string if no bracket ID.
270	  status: TaskStatus;
271	  content: string;         // text after the bracket (without doc links, carry links)
272	  subtasks: Task[];        // nested children
273	  indent: number;          // character count of leading whitespace
274	  links: DocLink[];        // (📄 [[...]]) refs
275	  history: Wikilink[];     // ← [[...]] chain (carried-from)
276	  forward?: Wikilink;      // → [[...]] (deferred-to)
277	  completion?: string;     // "2026-03-31" from ✅
278	  cancellation?: string;   // "2026-03-31" from ❌
279	  cancelReason?: string;   // text after " - " following ❌ date
280	  isLabel: boolean;        // true for group labels: - **[N] text**
281	  isStrikethrough: boolean; // true if content is wrapped in ~~
282	}
283	
284	export interface TextNode extends BaseNode {
285	  type: 'text';
286	  content: string;         // the line text
287	}
288	
289	export interface HeadingNode extends BaseNode {
290	  type: 'heading';
291	  level: number;           // 3 for ###
292	  content: string;         // heading text
293	}
294	
295	export interface TableNode extends BaseNode {
296	  type: 'table';
297	  headers: string[];       // column headers
298	  rows: string[][];        // cell values (preserve dataview expressions verbatim)
299	  rawLines: string[];      // all original lines for roundtrip
300	}
301	
302	export interface BlankNode extends BaseNode {
303	  type: 'blank';
304	}
305	
306	export type Node = Task | TextNode | HeadingNode | TableNode | BlankNode;
307	
308	// ── Section ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
309	
310	export interface Section {
311	  heading: string;         // "Tasks", "Log", "Session Work"
312	  level: number;           // 2 for ##
313	  body: Node[];            // child nodes
314	  line: number;            // 1-based line of the ## heading
315	  raw: string;             // original heading line
316	}
317	
318	// ── Document ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
319	
320	export interface Document {
321	  frontmatter: Record<string, any>;  // parsed YAML
322	  frontmatterRaw: string;            // original --- block for roundtrip
323	  preamble: Node[];                  // nodes between frontmatter and first ##
324	  sections: Section[];
325	  raw: string;                       // full original text
326	}
327	
328	// ── Filters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
329	
330	export interface TaskFilter {
331	  status?: TaskStatus | TaskStatus[];
332	  search?: string;                   // case-insensitive substring
333	  hasDocLinks?: boolean;
334	  hasHistory?: boolean;
335	}
336	
337	export interface MoveOpts {
338	  under?: string;     // reparent under this task ID
339	  toTop?: boolean;    // promote sub-task to top-level
340	  before?: string;    // insert before this task ID
341	  after?: string;     // insert after this task ID
342	}
343	```
344	
345	### 2. `parser.ts`
346	
347	Single export: `parse(markdown: string): Document`
348	
349	**Algorithm:**
350	
351	1. Split input into lines. Store as `raw` on the Document.
352	2. Detect frontmatter: find first `---` and second `---`. Extract raw block. Parse via `parseFrontmatter()` from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts`.
353	3. Walk remaining lines. Track current section (null until first `##`).
354	4. For each line, classify:
355	   - `## Heading` → start new Section (level 2). Close previous section.
356	   - `### Heading` → HeadingNode inside current section body (level 3+).
357	   - Blank line → BlankNode.
358	   - `| ... |` → start or continue a TableNode (consecutive pipe lines form one table).
359	   - `- [x] ...` / `- [ ] ...` etc. → Task node (parse status, ID, content, links).
360	   - `  - [x] ...` → Sub-task. Attach to the most recent top-level task or label.
361	   - `- **[N] ...**` → Label task (isLabel: true).
362	   - Everything else → TextNode.
363	5. Lines before the first `##` (after frontmatter) go into `preamble`.
364	
365	**Task line parsing** (the most complex part):
366	
367	```
368	Pattern: /^(\s*)- (\[(.)\] )?(\*\*)?(?:\[(\d+(?:\.\d+)*)\]\s*)?(.+?)(\*\*)?$/
369	```
370	
371	But this is too brittle for a single regex. Instead, use sequential extraction:
372	
373	1. Measure indent (`indent`).
374	2. Check for label: starts with `- **[`.
375	3. Extract checkbox: `- [X] ` where X is any char. Map to TaskStatus.
376	4. Extract bracket ID: `[5]` or `[5.3]`.
377	5. Extract doc links: `(📄 [[path|N]] [[path|N]])`.
378	6. Extract carry-forward history: all `← [[path|alias]]` occurrences.
379	7. Extract forward link: `→ [[path|alias]]`.
380	8. Extract completion: `✅ YYYY-MM-DD`.
381	9. Extract cancellation: `❌ YYYY-MM-DD` with optional ` - reason`.
382	10. Detect strikethrough: content wrapped in `~~`.
383	11. Remaining text after all extractions = `content`.
384	
385	**Key implementation notes:**
386	
387	- Parse wikilinks with regex: `/\[\[([^\]|]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]/g`
388	- Doc link block regex: `/\(📄\s*((?:\[\[[^\]]+\]\]\s*)+)\)/`
389	- History links regex: `/←\s*\[\[([^\]|]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]/g`
390	- Forward link regex: `/→\s*\[\[([^\]|]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]/`
391	- Completion regex: `/✅\s*(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})/`
392	- Cancellation regex: `/❌\s*(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})(?:\s*-\s*(.+))?/`
393	- Strikethrough regex: `/^~~(.+)~~$/` on the content after ID extraction
394	- Table detection: line starts with `|`. Consecutive `|` lines are grouped. First `|` line = headers, second = separator (skip), rest = data rows.
395	
396	**Sub-task attachment:** When a task has indent > 0, find the last top-level task (indent 0) or label in the current section and push to its `subtasks[]`. If no parent exists, treat as standalone (defensive).
397	
398	### 3. `serializer.ts`
399	
400	Single export: `serialize(doc: Document): string`
401	
402	**Algorithm:**
403	
404	1. If doc is not dirty at all (no node has `dirty: true` and frontmatter unchanged), return `doc.raw`.
405	2. Otherwise, rebuild line by line:
406	   a. Emit frontmatter: if frontmatter changed, use `serializeFrontmatter()` from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts`. Otherwise emit `doc.frontmatterRaw`.
407	   b. Emit preamble nodes.
408	   c. For each section: emit heading line, then each body node.
409	3. For each node:
410	   - If `!node.dirty`, emit `node.raw`.
411	   - If `node.dirty`, reconstruct from structured fields:
412	     - **Task:** `{indent}- [{statusChar}] {labelOpen}[{id}] {content}{strikethrough}{docLinks}{history}{forward}{completion}{cancellation}{labelClose}`
413	     - **Text:** `- {content}` or just `{content}` depending on original format.
414	     - **Heading:** `{'#'.repeat(level)} {content}`
415	     - **Table:** reconstruct from headers + rows with pipe alignment.
416	     - **Blank:** empty string.
417	4. After emitting subtasks of a task (which come right after the parent in section body ordering), maintain original blank-line boundaries.
418	
419	**Task serialization detail:**
420	
421	```typescript
422	function serializeTask(task: Task): string {
423	  const indent = ' '.repeat(task.indent);
424	  const statusChar = STATUS_TO_CHAR[task.status];
425	  
426	  if (task.isLabel) {
427	    // - **[N] content**
428	    let line = `${indent}- **[${task.id}] ${task.content}`;
429	    if (task.forward) line += ` → [[${task.forward.path}${task.forward.alias ? '|' + task.forward.alias : ''}]]`;
430	    line += '**';
431	    return line;
432	  }
433	  
434	  let line = `${indent}- [${statusChar}] `;
435	  if (task.id) line += `[${task.id}] `;
436	  
437	  let content = task.content;
438	  if (task.isStrikethrough) content = `~~${content}~~`;
439	  line += content;
440	  
441	  // Doc links
442	  if (task.links.length > 0) {
443	    const refs = task.links.map(l => `[[${l.path}|${l.number}]]`).join(' ');
444	    line += ` (📄 ${refs})`;
445	  }
446	  
447	  // History
448	  for (const h of task.history) {
449	    line += ` ← [[${h.path}${h.alias ? '|' + h.alias : ''}]]`;
450	  }
451	  
452	  // Forward
453	  if (task.forward) {
454	    line += ` → [[${task.forward.path}${task.forward.alias ? '|' + task.forward.alias : ''}]]`;
455	  }
456	  
457	  // Completion/cancellation
458	  if (task.completion) line += ` ✅ ${task.completion}`;
459	  if (task.cancellation) {
460	    line += ` ❌ ${task.cancellation}`;
461	    if (task.cancelReason) line += ` - ${task.cancelReason}`;
462	  }
463	  
464	  return line;
465	}
466	```
467	
468	**Roundtrip guarantee:** The key mechanism is the `dirty` flag. Parse sets `dirty: false` on every node. Operations that mutate a node set `dirty: true`. Serializer uses `raw` for clean nodes, reconstructs only dirty nodes. This means formatting, whitespace, and any unrecognized syntax within clean nodes is preserved exactly.
469	
470	### 4. `operations.ts`
471	
472	Functions that take and return `Document`. All mutations create new objects (immutable pattern) and set `dirty: true` on modified nodes.
473	
474	**Imports:** types from `types.ts`, `parse` from `parser.ts` (for content string → node conversion).
475	
476	#### Section operations
477	
478	```typescript
479	export function findSection(doc: Document, name: string): Section | undefined
480	```
481	Case-insensitive match on `section.heading`.
482	
483	```typescript
484	export function appendToSection(doc: Document, name: string, content: string): Document
485	```
486	Parse `content` as node(s). Append to section body (before trailing blank nodes). If section doesn't exist, create it at the end with level 2.
487	
488	```typescript
489	export function replaceSectionContent(doc: Document, name: string, content: string): Document
490	```
491	Parse `content` as nodes. Replace all body nodes in the section. Preserve the heading.
492	
493	#### Task operations
494	
495	```typescript
496	export function findTask(doc: Document, id: string): Task | undefined
497	```
498	Recursive search across all sections. Checks top-level tasks and their subtasks. Match on `task.id === id`.
499	
500	```typescript
501	export function listTasks(doc: Document, filter?: TaskFilter): Task[]
502	```
503	Flatten all tasks (including subtasks) across the document. Apply filter if provided:
504	- `status` — match single or array of statuses
505	- `search` — case-insensitive substring match on content
506	- `hasDocLinks` — true if links.length > 0
507	- `hasHistory` — true if history.length > 0
508	
509	```typescript
510	export function addTask(doc: Document, content: string, parent?: string): Document
511	```
512	If `parent` is provided, find parent task and add as subtask with next dotted ID. Otherwise add as top-level task in "Tasks" section with next integer ID. Creates the Tasks section if it doesn't exist.
513	
514	```typescript
515	export function setTaskStatus(doc: Document, id: string, status: TaskStatus): Document
516	```
517	Find task by ID, update its status, set `dirty: true`.
518	
519	```typescript
520	export function completeTask(doc: Document, id: string): Document
521	```
522	Set status to `done`. Add `completion` date (today's date in YYYY-MM-DD). Set `dirty: true`.
523	
524	```typescript
525	export function moveTask(doc: Document, id: string, opts: MoveOpts): Document
526	```
527	Handle three cases:
528	- `opts.under` — remove task from current position, add as subtask under target parent. Reassign ID to `{parent}.{nextSub}`.
529	- `opts.toTop` — remove subtask, add as new top-level task. Assign next integer ID.
530	- `opts.before` / `opts.after` — reorder within the same level.
531	
532	```typescript
533	export function linkDoc(doc: Document, taskId: string, docPath: string): Document
534	```
535	Find task. Add DocLink with next number. Set `dirty: true`.
536	
537	```typescript
538	export function unlinkDoc(doc: Document, taskId: string, docPath: string): Document
539	```
540	Find task. Remove matching DocLink. Renumber remaining links. Set `dirty: true`.
541	
542	```typescript
543	export function getNextTaskId(doc: Document, parentId?: string): string
544	```
545	If `parentId`, scan subtasks of that parent and return `{parentId}.{max+1}`. Otherwise scan all top-level tasks and labels for max integer ID, return `max+1` as string.
546	
547	#### Carry-forward
548	
549	```typescript
550	export function carryForward(source: Document, target: Document, targetDate: string): Document
551	```
552	1. Find all incomplete tasks (status `todo`, `in_progress`, `urgent`, `question`) in source's Tasks section. Include labels with incomplete subtasks.
553	2. In source: set status to `deferred`, wrap content in strikethrough, add forward link `→ [[notes/daily/{targetDate}|{MM-DD}]]`. Mark dirty.
554	3. In target: add each task with new sequential IDs, strip old bracket IDs from content, add history link `← [[notes/daily/{sourceDate}|{MM-DD}]]`. Preserve existing history chain.
555	4. Return the modified target document. Caller is responsible for serializing both source and target.
556	
557	Note: this function also needs to return the modified source. Signature should be:
558	
559	```typescript
560	export function carryForward(
561	  source: Document, 
562	  target: Document, 
563	  targetDate: string
564	): { source: Document; target: Document }
565	```
566	
567	### 5. `index.ts`
568	
569	```typescript
570	export * from './types.js';
571	export { parse } from './parser.js';
572	export { serialize } from './serializer.js';
573	export {
574	  findSection,
575	  appendToSection,
576	  replaceSectionContent,
577	  findTask,
578	  listTasks,
579	  addTask,
580	  setTaskStatus,
581	  completeTask,
582	  moveTask,
583	  linkDoc,
584	  unlinkDoc,
585	  getNextTaskId,
586	  carryForward,
587	} from './operations.js';
588	```
589	
590	## Edge Cases
591	
592	| Case | Expected behavior |
593	|------|-------------------|
594	| No frontmatter | `frontmatter = {}`, `frontmatterRaw = ""`, body starts at line 1 |
595	| Frontmatter only (no body) | Sections = [], preamble = [] |
596	| Empty section (just `## Heading` followed by `## Next`) | Section with empty body |
597	| Task with no bracket ID | `id = ""`, still parseable. `getNextTaskId` ignores these. |
598	| Task with no checkbox (just `- text`) | Parsed as TextNode, not Task |
599	| Sub-task with no parent above it | Treat as top-level task (defensive, log warning) |
600	| Consecutive blank lines | Each is a separate BlankNode, preserved in roundtrip |
601	| Table with dataview expressions | Cells contain `$= ...` backtick expressions. Stored verbatim in `rows[][]`. |
602	| Wikilink inside regular text | TextNode stores raw line. Wikilinks not extracted from non-task nodes. |
603	| Mixed `\r\n` and `\n` line endings | Normalize to `\n` on parse. Serialize always uses `\n`. |
604	| Label with nested sub-tasks | Label's `subtasks[]` populated. Labels have no checkbox but do have IDs. |
605	| Deferred task with long history chain | All `← [[...]]` entries captured in `history[]` in order |
606	| Task with both doc links AND history | Doc links extracted first (they appear before history in the line) |
607	| Trailing newline at end of file | Preserved as final BlankNode |
608	| Content between frontmatter and first `##` | Stored in `preamble` (observed: none in current vault, but handle defensively) |
609	
610	## Acceptance Criteria
611	
612	1. **Roundtrip identity.** For every daily note in `vault/notes/daily/`, `serialize(parse(readFile(path))) === readFile(path)` after normalizing line endings to `\n`.
613	2. **Task parsing accuracy.** Parse `2026-04-01.md` and verify:
614	   - 7 top-level tasks found (IDs 1-7)
615	   - Task 5 has 3 subtasks (5.1, 5.2, 5.3)
616	   - Task 6 has status `done` (was `[x]`)
617	   - Task 1 has 8 history entries (carry-forward chain)
618	   - Task 4 has 1 doc link
619	   - Task 6 has 1 doc link
620	3. **Status mapping.** All 7 status characters map correctly and roundtrip.
621	4. **Section operations.** `findSection`, `appendToSection`, `replaceSectionContent` work on parsed documents and produce correct output when serialized.
622	5. **Task mutations.** `addTask`, `setTaskStatus`, `completeTask` produce correct output. Only the mutated node's line changes; all other lines remain identical.
623	6. **Doc link operations.** `linkDoc` adds with correct numbering. `unlinkDoc` removes and renumbers.
624	7. **Label parsing.** `- **[3] OT-1598: ...**` parsed as Task with `isLabel: true`.
625	8. **Deferred task parsing.** Strikethrough content, forward link, and history all extracted correctly.
626	9. **Table preservation.** Dataview `$= ...` expressions in table cells survive roundtrip unchanged.
627	10. **Zero dependencies.** Only imports from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts` and TypeScript built-ins.
628	
629	## Execution Order
630	
631	Build and verify incrementally. Each step must pass before the next.
632	
633	| Step | File | Deliverable | Depends on |
634	|------|------|-------------|------------|
635	| 1 | `types.ts` | All interfaces, types, constants | nothing |
636	| 2 | `parser.ts` | `parse()` function | types.ts, frontmatter.ts |
637	| 3 | `serializer.ts` | `serialize()` function | types.ts, frontmatter.ts |
638	| 4 | roundtrip test | Verify `serialize(parse(text)) === text` on real vault files | parser.ts, serializer.ts |
639	| 5 | `operations.ts` | All section + task + carry-forward ops | parser.ts, serializer.ts |
640	| 6 | `index.ts` | Re-exports | all above |
641	| 7 | operations tests | Verify mutations produce correct output | operations.ts |
642	
643	**Step 4 is the gate.** Do not proceed to operations until roundtrip is proven on at least 3 daily notes (2026-04-01, 2026-03-31, 2026-03-29), 1 weekly (2026-W13), and 1 meeting note (2026-03-24-ops-checkin).
644	
645	## Testing Strategy
646	
647	Tests should use real vault file content as fixtures (copy the content into test strings, do not read from disk at test time -- keep tests self-contained). Key test categories:
648	
649	1. **Roundtrip tests** -- parse then serialize, assert identity
650	2. **Parse structure tests** -- assert correct node counts, types, task IDs, statuses
651	3. **Task field extraction tests** -- doc links, history chains, completion dates, labels
652	4. **Operation tests** -- addTask, setTaskStatus, completeTask, linkDoc, unlinkDoc, moveTask
653	5. **Edge case tests** -- no frontmatter, empty sections, sub-task without parent, mixed line endings
654	
655	## Notes
656	
657	- The `dirty` flag pattern is critical for roundtrip safety. Do not skip it. The alternative (always reconstructing from fields) will break on any unrecognized syntax or unusual whitespace.
658	- The parser does NOT need to understand wikilinks in arbitrary text. It only extracts structured wikilinks from task lines (doc links, history, forward). All other wikilinks are preserved as raw text.
659	- Meeting notes have action items with bold name prefixes (`- [ ] **Name** — text`). These parse as regular tasks with the bold markup inside `content`. No special handling needed -- the content field preserves the original formatting.
660	- The `preamble` field handles the (currently theoretical) case of content between frontmatter and first section. In practice all vault notes go straight from frontmatter to `## Summary`, but the parser should be defensive.
661

file.content:
---
type: task
id: OT-0013
status: pending
priority: high
created: 2026-04-01T16:43:00+08:00
depends: []
blocks: []
---

# Obsidian-Flavored Markdown Parsing Library

## Objective

Build `src/libs/obsidian-md/` -- a zero-dependency, roundtrip-safe parser/serializer for the project's Obsidian vault markdown. Three layers: parse/serialize, typed models, AST operations. This is a NEW library -- the existing `src/libs/sections.ts` and `src/tools/obsidian/*.ts` stay untouched. Migration is a separate task.

## Architecture

```
src/libs/obsidian-md/
  index.ts          -- re-exports everything
  types.ts          -- all interfaces, types, enums
  parser.ts         -- parse(markdown) -> Document
  serializer.ts     -- serialize(Document) -> markdown
  operations.ts     -- section ops, task ops, carry-forward
```

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    MD["Markdown string"] --> P["parse()"]
    P --> AST["Document AST"]
    AST --> OPS["operations.ts"]
    OPS --> AST2["Modified AST"]
    AST2 --> S["serialize()"]
    S --> MD2["Markdown string"]

    AST --> FM["frontmatter: Record"]
    AST --> SEC["sections: Section[]"]
    SEC --> NODES["body: Node[]"]
    NODES --> TASK["TaskNode"]
    NODES --> TEXT["TextNode"]
    NODES --> TABLE["TableNode"]
    NODES --> DV["DataviewNode"]
```

## Constraints

- **Zero external dependencies.** No remark, marked, yaml, etc. Use the existing `src/libs/frontmatter.ts` for frontmatter parsing/serialization (it already handles YAML frontmatter, inline arrays, multi-line lists, wikilink quoting).
- **Roundtrip-safe.** `serialize(parse(text)) === text` for unchanged documents. Every node stores its `raw` source line(s). Serializer emits `raw` unless the node was mutated (tracked via a `dirty` flag).
- **TypeScript, Bun runtime.** No browser compat needed.
- **All patterns from actual vault notes must parse correctly.** See "Markdown Patterns" section below.

## Markdown Patterns (observed in vault)

These are the actual patterns found in daily notes, weekly summaries, and meeting notes. The parser MUST handle all of them.

### Frontmatter

```yaml
---
id: daily-20260401
title: 2026-04-01
tags: [daily]
connected:
  - "[[notes/daily/2026-03-31]]"
---
```

Reuse `parseFrontmatter()` and `serializeFrontmatter()` from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts`. Do NOT reimplement.

### Headings

```markdown
## Tasks
## Log
## Session Work
### Odelon Miranda
```

Level 2 (`##`) are section boundaries. Level 3 (`###`) are subsection headers within a section body.

### Tasks with bracket IDs

```markdown
- [ ] [1] Task text
- [x] [6] Completed task ✅ 2026-03-31
- [/] [3] In-progress task
- [>] [1] ~~Deferred text~~ → [[notes/daily/2026-04-01|04-01]]
- [-] [10] [log] Cancelled task ❌ 2026-03-31 - reason
- [!] [2] Urgent task
- [?] [4] Question task
```

### Sub-tasks (indented, dotted IDs)

```markdown
- [ ] [5] Parent task
  - [ ] [5.1] Sub-task one
  - [x] [5.2] Sub-task two
  - [ ] [5.3] Sub-task three
```

Two-space indent. IDs are `{parent}.{child}` format. Sub-tasks can have carry-forward links and doc links just like top-level tasks.

### Group labels (bold, non-checkbox)

```markdown
- **[3] OT-1598: Continue working on auth**
- **[4] ~~Moved group~~ → [[notes/daily/2026-03-24|03-24]]**
```

These are task group headers. They use bold `**`, have bracket IDs, but no checkbox. They can have sub-tasks beneath them.

### Doc link references

```markdown
- [x] [9] Task text (📄 [[notes/docs/prompt-builder-engine|1]] [[notes/docs/prompt-builder-phase1-session2|5]])
```

The `(📄 ...)` block contains numbered wikilinks. Multiple docs are space-separated inside the parens.

### Carry-forward chains

```markdown
- [ ] [1] Task text ← [[notes/daily/2026-03-24|03-24]] ← [[notes/daily/2026-03-25|03-25]]
```

Each `← [[path|alias]]` is a history entry showing which daily note the task was carried from. Forward arrows `→ [[path|alias]]` indicate where a deferred task was sent.

### Deferred tasks (strikethrough + arrow)

```markdown
- [>] [1] ~~Original text ← [[...]] ← [[...]]~~ → [[notes/daily/2026-04-01|04-01]]
```

The entire content is wrapped in `~~strikethrough~~`, followed by `→` and the target link.

### Completion markers

```markdown
- [x] [9] Task text ✅ 2026-03-31
- [-] [10] Cancelled task ❌ 2026-03-31 - reason
```

Completion date after checkmark emoji. Cancellation uses ❌ with optional reason after ` - `.

### Dataview inline JS

```markdown
| Tasks completed | `$= const t = dv.current().file.tasks.where(t => t.section.subpath === "Tasks"); dv.span(t.where(t => t.completed).length + "/" + t.length)` |
```

These are backtick-wrapped JS expressions starting with `$=`. They appear inside table cells. The parser must preserve them verbatim.

### Wikilinks

```markdown
[[notes/daily/2026-03-31]]
[[notes/daily/2026-03-31|03-31]]
[[notes/meetings/2026-03-23-skill-demo-mike-sev]]
```

Path only, or path with `|alias`.

### Tables

```markdown
| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| Tasks completed | value |
```

Standard markdown tables. Preserve alignment and cell content exactly.

### Regular list items

```markdown
- 7:54 AM — Dev session: freddie-ai — description
- CLI-only for all tool calls — detail
- **Test result — description:** detail text
```

Non-task list items (no checkbox). Used in Log, Decisions, Learned, Notes sections.

### Subsection headers within sections

```markdown
## Session Work
### Tested /create-track pipeline end-to-end
### Dev session: Phase 1 Suno Style Builder
```

### Focus areas line

```markdown
**Focus areas:** (pending)
**Focus areas:** Suno vocal production, music cards
```

Special bold line in Summary section.

### Meeting note patterns

```markdown
## Action Items
- [ ] **Odelon** — Submit PR for 60-min token refresh cycle
- [ ] **Team** — Investigate locking AirTable view
```

Bold name prefix on action items.

## File-by-File Spec

### 1. `types.ts`

All types and interfaces. No logic, no imports beyond TypeScript built-ins.

```typescript
// ── Status ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export type TaskStatus = 'todo' | 'done' | 'in_progress' | 'deferred' | 'cancelled' | 'urgent' | 'question';

export const STATUS_CHARS: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
  ' ': 'todo',
  'x': 'done',
  'X': 'done',
  '/': 'in_progress',
  '>': 'deferred',
  '-': 'cancelled',
  '!': 'urgent',
  '?': 'question',
};

export const STATUS_TO_CHAR: Record<TaskStatus, string> = {
  todo: ' ',
  done: 'x',
  in_progress: '/',
  deferred: '>',
  cancelled: '-',
  urgent: '!',
  question: '?',
};

// ── Wikilinks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export interface Wikilink {
  path: string;        // "notes/daily/2026-03-31"
  alias?: string;      // "03-31"
}

export interface DocLink {
  path: string;        // "notes/docs/prompt-builder-engine"
  number: number;      // 1
}

// ── Nodes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export type NodeType = 'task' | 'text' | 'heading' | 'table' | 'blank';

export interface BaseNode {
  type: NodeType;
  line: number;        // 1-based source line number
  raw: string;         // original line text (or multi-line for tables)
  dirty: boolean;      // true if mutated since parse
}

export interface Task extends BaseNode {
  type: 'task';
  id: string;              // "5", "5.3" — from [N] or [N.M] bracket. Empty string if no bracket ID.
  status: TaskStatus;
  content: string;         // text after the bracket (without doc links, carry links)
  subtasks: Task[];        // nested children
  indent: number;          // character count of leading whitespace
  links: DocLink[];        // (📄 [[...]]) refs
  history: Wikilink[];     // ← [[...]] chain (carried-from)
  forward?: Wikilink;      // → [[...]] (deferred-to)
  completion?: string;     // "2026-03-31" from ✅
  cancellation?: string;   // "2026-03-31" from ❌
  cancelReason?: string;   // text after " - " following ❌ date
  isLabel: boolean;        // true for group labels: - **[N] text**
  isStrikethrough: boolean; // true if content is wrapped in ~~
}

export interface TextNode extends BaseNode {
  type: 'text';
  content: string;         // the line text
}

export interface HeadingNode extends BaseNode {
  type: 'heading';
  level: number;           // 3 for ###
  content: string;         // heading text
}

export interface TableNode extends BaseNode {
  type: 'table';
  headers: string[];       // column headers
  rows: string[][];        // cell values (preserve dataview expressions verbatim)
  rawLines: string[];      // all original lines for roundtrip
}

export interface BlankNode extends BaseNode {
  type: 'blank';
}

export type Node = Task | TextNode | HeadingNode | TableNode | BlankNode;

// ── Section ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export interface Section {
  heading: string;         // "Tasks", "Log", "Session Work"
  level: number;           // 2 for ##
  body: Node[];            // child nodes
  line: number;            // 1-based line of the ## heading
  raw: string;             // original heading line
}

// ── Document ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export interface Document {
  frontmatter: Record<string, any>;  // parsed YAML
  frontmatterRaw: string;            // original --- block for roundtrip
  preamble: Node[];                  // nodes between frontmatter and first ##
  sections: Section[];
  raw: string;                       // full original text
}

// ── Filters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export interface TaskFilter {
  status?: TaskStatus | TaskStatus[];
  search?: string;                   // case-insensitive substring
  hasDocLinks?: boolean;
  hasHistory?: boolean;
}

export interface MoveOpts {
  under?: string;     // reparent under this task ID
  toTop?: boolean;    // promote sub-task to top-level
  before?: string;    // insert before this task ID
  after?: string;     // insert after this task ID
}
```

### 2. `parser.ts`

Single export: `parse(markdown: string): Document`

**Algorithm:**

1. Split input into lines. Store as `raw` on the Document.
2. Detect frontmatter: find first `---` and second `---`. Extract raw block. Parse via `parseFrontmatter()` from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts`.
3. Walk remaining lines. Track current section (null until first `##`).
4. For each line, classify:
   - `## Heading` → start new Section (level 2). Close previous section.
   - `### Heading` → HeadingNode inside current section body (level 3+).
   - Blank line → BlankNode.
   - `| ... |` → start or continue a TableNode (consecutive pipe lines form one table).
   - `- [x] ...` / `- [ ] ...` etc. → Task node (parse status, ID, content, links).
   - `  - [x] ...` → Sub-task. Attach to the most recent top-level task or label.
   - `- **[N] ...**` → Label task (isLabel: true).
   - Everything else → TextNode.
5. Lines before the first `##` (after frontmatter) go into `preamble`.

**Task line parsing** (the most complex part):

```
Pattern: /^(\s*)- (\[(.)\] )?(\*\*)?(?:\[(\d+(?:\.\d+)*)\]\s*)?(.+?)(\*\*)?$/
```

But this is too brittle for a single regex. Instead, use sequential extraction:

1. Measure indent (`indent`).
2. Check for label: starts with `- **[`.
3. Extract checkbox: `- [X] ` where X is any char. Map to TaskStatus.
4. Extract bracket ID: `[5]` or `[5.3]`.
5. Extract doc links: `(📄 [[path|N]] [[path|N]])`.
6. Extract carry-forward history: all `← [[path|alias]]` occurrences.
7. Extract forward link: `→ [[path|alias]]`.
8. Extract completion: `✅ YYYY-MM-DD`.
9. Extract cancellation: `❌ YYYY-MM-DD` with optional ` - reason`.
10. Detect strikethrough: content wrapped in `~~`.
11. Remaining text after all extractions = `content`.

**Key implementation notes:**

- Parse wikilinks with regex: `/\[\[([^\]|]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]/g`
- Doc link block regex: `/\(📄\s*((?:\[\[[^\]]+\]\]\s*)+)\)/`
- History links regex: `/←\s*\[\[([^\]|]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]/g`
- Forward link regex: `/→\s*\[\[([^\]|]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]/`
- Completion regex: `/✅\s*(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})/`
- Cancellation regex: `/❌\s*(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})(?:\s*-\s*(.+))?/`
- Strikethrough regex: `/^~~(.+)~~$/` on the content after ID extraction
- Table detection: line starts with `|`. Consecutive `|` lines are grouped. First `|` line = headers, second = separator (skip), rest = data rows.

**Sub-task attachment:** When a task has indent > 0, find the last top-level task (indent 0) or label in the current section and push to its `subtasks[]`. If no parent exists, treat as standalone (defensive).

### 3. `serializer.ts`

Single export: `serialize(doc: Document): string`

**Algorithm:**

1. If doc is not dirty at all (no node has `dirty: true` and frontmatter unchanged), return `doc.raw`.
2. Otherwise, rebuild line by line:
   a. Emit frontmatter: if frontmatter changed, use `serializeFrontmatter()` from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts`. Otherwise emit `doc.frontmatterRaw`.
   b. Emit preamble nodes.
   c. For each section: emit heading line, then each body node.
3. For each node:
   - If `!node.dirty`, emit `node.raw`.
   - If `node.dirty`, reconstruct from structured fields:
     - **Task:** `{indent}- [{statusChar}] {labelOpen}[{id}] {content}{strikethrough}{docLinks}{history}{forward}{completion}{cancellation}{labelClose}`
     - **Text:** `- {content}` or just `{content}` depending on original format.
     - **Heading:** `{'#'.repeat(level)} {content}`
     - **Table:** reconstruct from headers + rows with pipe alignment.
     - **Blank:** empty string.
4. After emitting subtasks of a task (which come right after the parent in section body ordering), maintain original blank-line boundaries.

**Task serialization detail:**

```typescript
function serializeTask(task: Task): string {
  const indent = ' '.repeat(task.indent);
  const statusChar = STATUS_TO_CHAR[task.status];
  
  if (task.isLabel) {
    // - **[N] content**
    let line = `${indent}- **[${task.id}] ${task.content}`;
    if (task.forward) line += ` → [[${task.forward.path}${task.forward.alias ? '|' + task.forward.alias : ''}]]`;
    line += '**';
    return line;
  }
  
  let line = `${indent}- [${statusChar}] `;
  if (task.id) line += `[${task.id}] `;
  
  let content = task.content;
  if (task.isStrikethrough) content = `~~${content}~~`;
  line += content;
  
  // Doc links
  if (task.links.length > 0) {
    const refs = task.links.map(l => `[[${l.path}|${l.number}]]`).join(' ');
    line += ` (📄 ${refs})`;
  }
  
  // History
  for (const h of task.history) {
    line += ` ← [[${h.path}${h.alias ? '|' + h.alias : ''}]]`;
  }
  
  // Forward
  if (task.forward) {
    line += ` → [[${task.forward.path}${task.forward.alias ? '|' + task.forward.alias : ''}]]`;
  }
  
  // Completion/cancellation
  if (task.completion) line += ` ✅ ${task.completion}`;
  if (task.cancellation) {
    line += ` ❌ ${task.cancellation}`;
    if (task.cancelReason) line += ` - ${task.cancelReason}`;
  }
  
  return line;
}
```

**Roundtrip guarantee:** The key mechanism is the `dirty` flag. Parse sets `dirty: false` on every node. Operations that mutate a node set `dirty: true`. Serializer uses `raw` for clean nodes, reconstructs only dirty nodes. This means formatting, whitespace, and any unrecognized syntax within clean nodes is preserved exactly.

### 4. `operations.ts`

Functions that take and return `Document`. All mutations create new objects (immutable pattern) and set `dirty: true` on modified nodes.

**Imports:** types from `types.ts`, `parse` from `parser.ts` (for content string → node conversion).

#### Section operations

```typescript
export function findSection(doc: Document, name: string): Section | undefined
```
Case-insensitive match on `section.heading`.

```typescript
export function appendToSection(doc: Document, name: string, content: string): Document
```
Parse `content` as node(s). Append to section body (before trailing blank nodes). If section doesn't exist, create it at the end with level 2.

```typescript
export function replaceSectionContent(doc: Document, name: string, content: string): Document
```
Parse `content` as nodes. Replace all body nodes in the section. Preserve the heading.

#### Task operations

```typescript
export function findTask(doc: Document, id: string): Task | undefined
```
Recursive search across all sections. Checks top-level tasks and their subtasks. Match on `task.id === id`.

```typescript
export function listTasks(doc: Document, filter?: TaskFilter): Task[]
```
Flatten all tasks (including subtasks) across the document. Apply filter if provided:
- `status` — match single or array of statuses
- `search` — case-insensitive substring match on content
- `hasDocLinks` — true if links.length > 0
- `hasHistory` — true if history.length > 0

```typescript
export function addTask(doc: Document, content: string, parent?: string): Document
```
If `parent` is provided, find parent task and add as subtask with next dotted ID. Otherwise add as top-level task in "Tasks" section with next integer ID. Creates the Tasks section if it doesn't exist.

```typescript
export function setTaskStatus(doc: Document, id: string, status: TaskStatus): Document
```
Find task by ID, update its status, set `dirty: true`.

```typescript
export function completeTask(doc: Document, id: string): Document
```
Set status to `done`. Add `completion` date (today's date in YYYY-MM-DD). Set `dirty: true`.

```typescript
export function moveTask(doc: Document, id: string, opts: MoveOpts): Document
```
Handle three cases:
- `opts.under` — remove task from current position, add as subtask under target parent. Reassign ID to `{parent}.{nextSub}`.
- `opts.toTop` — remove subtask, add as new top-level task. Assign next integer ID.
- `opts.before` / `opts.after` — reorder within the same level.

```typescript
export function linkDoc(doc: Document, taskId: string, docPath: string): Document
```
Find task. Add DocLink with next number. Set `dirty: true`.

```typescript
export function unlinkDoc(doc: Document, taskId: string, docPath: string): Document
```
Find task. Remove matching DocLink. Renumber remaining links. Set `dirty: true`.

```typescript
export function getNextTaskId(doc: Document, parentId?: string): string
```
If `parentId`, scan subtasks of that parent and return `{parentId}.{max+1}`. Otherwise scan all top-level tasks and labels for max integer ID, return `max+1` as string.

#### Carry-forward

```typescript
export function carryForward(source: Document, target: Document, targetDate: string): Document
```
1. Find all incomplete tasks (status `todo`, `in_progress`, `urgent`, `question`) in source's Tasks section. Include labels with incomplete subtasks.
2. In source: set status to `deferred`, wrap content in strikethrough, add forward link `→ [[notes/daily/{targetDate}|{MM-DD}]]`. Mark dirty.
3. In target: add each task with new sequential IDs, strip old bracket IDs from content, add history link `← [[notes/daily/{sourceDate}|{MM-DD}]]`. Preserve existing history chain.
4. Return the modified target document. Caller is responsible for serializing both source and target.

Note: this function also needs to return the modified source. Signature should be:

```typescript
export function carryForward(
  source: Document, 
  target: Document, 
  targetDate: string
): { source: Document; target: Document }
```

### 5. `index.ts`

```typescript
export * from './types.js';
export { parse } from './parser.js';
export { serialize } from './serializer.js';
export {
  findSection,
  appendToSection,
  replaceSectionContent,
  findTask,
  listTasks,
  addTask,
  setTaskStatus,
  completeTask,
  moveTask,
  linkDoc,
  unlinkDoc,
  getNextTaskId,
  carryForward,
} from './operations.js';
```

## Edge Cases

| Case | Expected behavior |
|------|-------------------|
| No frontmatter | `frontmatter = {}`, `frontmatterRaw = ""`, body starts at line 1 |
| Frontmatter only (no body) | Sections = [], preamble = [] |
| Empty section (just `## Heading` followed by `## Next`) | Section with empty body |
| Task with no bracket ID | `id = ""`, still parseable. `getNextTaskId` ignores these. |
| Task with no checkbox (just `- text`) | Parsed as TextNode, not Task |
| Sub-task with no parent above it | Treat as top-level task (defensive, log warning) |
| Consecutive blank lines | Each is a separate BlankNode, preserved in roundtrip |
| Table with dataview expressions | Cells contain `$= ...` backtick expressions. Stored verbatim in `rows[][]`. |
| Wikilink inside regular text | TextNode stores raw line. Wikilinks not extracted from non-task nodes. |
| Mixed `\r\n` and `\n` line endings | Normalize to `\n` on parse. Serialize always uses `\n`. |
| Label with nested sub-tasks | Label's `subtasks[]` populated. Labels have no checkbox but do have IDs. |
| Deferred task with long history chain | All `← [[...]]` entries captured in `history[]` in order |
| Task with both doc links AND history | Doc links extracted first (they appear before history in the line) |
| Trailing newline at end of file | Preserved as final BlankNode |
| Content between frontmatter and first `##` | Stored in `preamble` (observed: none in current vault, but handle defensively) |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Roundtrip identity.** For every daily note in `vault/notes/daily/`, `serialize(parse(readFile(path))) === readFile(path)` after normalizing line endings to `\n`.
2. **Task parsing accuracy.** Parse `2026-04-01.md` and verify:
   - 7 top-level tasks found (IDs 1-7)
   - Task 5 has 3 subtasks (5.1, 5.2, 5.3)
   - Task 6 has status `done` (was `[x]`)
   - Task 1 has 8 history entries (carry-forward chain)
   - Task 4 has 1 doc link
   - Task 6 has 1 doc link
3. **Status mapping.** All 7 status characters map correctly and roundtrip.
4. **Section operations.** `findSection`, `appendToSection`, `replaceSectionContent` work on parsed documents and produce correct output when serialized.
5. **Task mutations.** `addTask`, `setTaskStatus`, `completeTask` produce correct output. Only the mutated node's line changes; all other lines remain identical.
6. **Doc link operations.** `linkDoc` adds with correct numbering. `unlinkDoc` removes and renumbers.
7. **Label parsing.** `- **[3] OT-1598: ...**` parsed as Task with `isLabel: true`.
8. **Deferred task parsing.** Strikethrough content, forward link, and history all extracted correctly.
9. **Table preservation.** Dataview `$= ...` expressions in table cells survive roundtrip unchanged.
10. **Zero dependencies.** Only imports from `src/libs/frontmatter.ts` and TypeScript built-ins.

## Execution Order

Build and verify incrementally. Each step must pass before the next.

| Step | File | Deliverable | Depends on |
|------|------|-------------|------------|
| 1 | `types.ts` | All interfaces, types, constants | nothing |
| 2 | `parser.ts` | `parse()` function | types.ts, frontmatter.ts |
| 3 | `serializer.ts` | `serialize()` function | types.ts, frontmatter.ts |
| 4 | roundtrip test | Verify `serialize(parse(text)) === text` on real vault files | parser.ts, serializer.ts |
| 5 | `operations.ts` | All section + task + carry-forward ops | parser.ts, serializer.ts |
| 6 | `index.ts` | Re-exports | all above |
| 7 | operations tests | Verify mutations produce correct output | operations.ts |

**Step 4 is the gate.** Do not proceed to operations until roundtrip is proven on at least 3 daily notes (2026-04-01, 2026-03-31, 2026-03-29), 1 weekly (2026-W13), and 1 meeting note (2026-03-24-ops-checkin).

## Testing Strategy

Tests should use real vault file content as fixtures (copy the content into test strings, do not read from disk at test time -- keep tests self-contained). Key test categories:

1. **Roundtrip tests** -- parse then serialize, assert identity
2. **Parse structure tests** -- assert correct node counts, types, task IDs, statuses
3. **Task field extraction tests** -- doc links, history chains, completion dates, labels
4. **Operation tests** -- addTask, setTaskStatus, completeTask, linkDoc, unlinkDoc, moveTask
5. **Edge case tests** -- no frontmatter, empty sections, sub-task without parent, mixed line endings

## Notes

- The `dirty` flag pattern is critical for roundtrip safety. Do not skip it. The alternative (always reconstructing from fields) will break on any unrecognized syntax or unusual whitespace.
- The parser does NOT need to understand wikilinks in arbitrary text. It only extracts structured wikilinks from task lines (doc links, history, forward). All other wikilinks are preserved as raw text.
- Meeting notes have action items with bold name prefixes (`- [ ] **Name** — text`). These parse as regular tasks with the bold markup inside `content`. No special handling needed -- the content field preserves the original formatting.
- The `preamble` field handles the (currently theoretical) case of content between frontmatter and first section. In practice all vault notes go straight from frontmatter to `## Summary`, but the parser should be defensive.

content.text:
Solid spec from McCall — 661 lines, every pattern catalogued, clear execution order with a roundtrip gate at step 4. 

Five files, built in order:

| Step | File | What |
|------|------|------|
| 1 | `types.ts` | All interfaces + status maps |
| 2 | `parser.ts` | `parse()` — frontmatter + line classification + sequential task extraction |
| 3 | `serializer.ts` | `serialize()` — dirty-flag roundtrip (emit `raw` for clean nodes) |
| 4 | **Gate** | Roundtrip test on 5 real vault files before proceeding |
| 5 | `operations.ts` | Section ops, task CRUD, carry-forward |
| 6 | `index.ts` | Re-exports |

The key design decision is the **dirty-flag pattern** — every node keeps its original `raw` text. Only mutated nodes get reconstructed. This is what makes roundtrip safety possible without an expensive full-fidelity markdown AST.

content.text:
I have everything I need. Here is the review.

---

## OT-0013 Code Review: obsidian-md Library

### Summary

The implementation is solid and closely follows the task spec. Types match 1:1. Parser, serializer, and operations all align with the documented architecture. The roundtrip-safety dirty-flag pattern is correctly implemented. Below are findings organized by severity.

---

### Critical (must fix)

None.

---

### Important (should fix)

**I-1. `lastTopLevelTask` is not reset on `##` heading within the same walk.**

`parser.ts` line 254 — `onSectionChange()` resets `lastTopLevelTask` when a new `##` section starts. However, `lastTopLevelTask` is a shared mutable variable across the entire parse loop. If a sub-heading (`###`) or text node appears between a parent task and a sub-task, the sub-task will still attach to the previous task. This is probably correct behavior for the vault's actual format, but consider whether a non-task node (like a text line or heading) appearing between a parent task and an indented task should break the attachment chain. Current behavior: it does not break it. If vault data ever has that pattern, the subtask silently attaches to the wrong parent.

Recommendation: Document this as a known behavior choice. If strictness is desired, reset `lastTopLevelTask` when a non-task, non-blank node is encountered.

**I-2. `carryForward` always sets carried task status to `'todo'`, losing original status nuance.**

`operations.ts` lines 485-496 — The status logic is confused. Line 485 does `task.status === 'deferred' ? 'todo' : task.status`, but by that point `task.status` was already mutated to `'deferred'` on line 465. So it always resolves to `'todo'`. Then line 496 unconditionally sets `newTask.status = 'todo'` again. The first assignment on line 485 is dead code.

The spec says "incomplete tasks (todo, in_progress, urgent, question)" should be carried. The intent appears to be that `in_progress` tasks remain `in_progress` after carry-forward, not reset to `todo`. Current code loses that distinction.

Recommendation: Capture the original status before mutating the source task, then assign it to `newTask`:
```
const originalStatus = task.status;
// ... mutate source ...
newTask.status = originalStatus;
```
Remove the redundant line 496.

**I-3. `serializeNode` for dirty tasks emits subtask lines joined with `\n`, but the outer `emitNodes` also handles subtasks separately.**

`serializer.ts` lines 78-85 — When a task is dirty, `serializeNode` returns the task line AND its subtask lines joined by `\n`. But this function is only called for non-task nodes in `emitNodes` (lines 147-161 handle task nodes directly). The task branch in `serializeNode` is actually dead code — it will never execute from the current call sites because `emitNodes` has an explicit `node.type === 'task'` check. This is not a bug since the dead path isn't reached, but it's confusing.

Recommendation: Remove the `'task'` case from `serializeNode`, or add a comment explaining it exists only as a safety fallback.

**I-4. `carryForward` carries labels but labels have no checkbox status.**

`operations.ts` line 439 — `shouldCarry` checks if `task.status` is in `incompleteStatuses`. Labels are parsed with `status: 'todo'` (the default). So a label with incomplete subtasks WILL be carried (matching the spec). But a label with ALL completed subtasks will also be carried, because the label itself has `status: 'todo'`. The spec says "Include labels with incomplete subtasks" — labels with no incomplete subtasks should be skipped.

Recommendation: Add a check for labels: if `task.isLabel && task.subtasks.every(s => !shouldCarry(s))`, skip carrying the label.

---

### Minor (nice to have)

**M-1. `parseTableCells` handles backtick nesting but not escaped backticks.**

`parser.ts` line 23 — The backtick toggle (`inBacktick = !inBacktick`) works for simple cases but would break on escaped backticks or nested double-backtick expressions. Given the vault only uses `$= ...` dataview expressions in single backticks, this is fine for now. Just noting the limitation.

**M-2. `as Task` casts are used where narrowing would suffice.**

Throughout `operations.ts` and `serializer.ts`, `(node as Task)` casts appear after `node.type === 'task'` checks. TypeScript's discriminated union narrowing should handle this automatically since `type` is the discriminant in the `Node` union. The casts are safe but unnecessary — `node` should narrow to `Task` after the type guard.

Tested: the `Node` type is `Task | TextNode | HeadingNode | TableNode | BlankNode` with `type` as discriminant. TypeScript will narrow correctly after `if (node.type === 'task')`.

**M-3. `getNextTaskId` only scans section body, not preamble.**

`operations.ts` line 196 — Tasks in the preamble (between frontmatter and first `##`) are excluded from the max-ID scan. The spec notes preamble tasks are theoretical ("none in current vault") so this is low risk, but for defensive completeness the preamble should be included.

**M-4. `parseNodes` helper creates a full Document to extract nodes.**

`operations.ts` line 124 — This re-parses content as a full document just to get nodes. Functional and correct, but creates throwaway frontmatter parsing, a throwaway `raw` string, etc. Not a performance concern at current scale, just slightly inelegant.

**M-5. No `DataviewNode` type.**

The task spec's architecture diagram shows a `DataviewNode` in the AST. The implementation preserves dataview expressions inside `TableNode` cells as raw strings, which is the right call — a separate node type would be over-engineering. But the spec and implementation disagree on this point. Worth noting for documentation alignment.

**M-6. `index.ts` re-exports match the spec exactly.** No issue here — just confirming.

---

### Architecture Alignment

| Spec requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| 5-file layout (types, parser, serializer, operations, index) | Match |
| Zero external dependencies | Match — only imports `frontmatter.ts` |
| Roundtrip via dirty flag | Match |
| `parse()` / `serialize()` single exports | Match |
| All 7 task statuses | Match |
| Task field extraction (id, links, history, forward, completion, cancellation) | Match |
| Label parsing (`- **[N] ...**`) | Match |
| Deferred strikethrough handling | Match |
| Sub-task dotted IDs | Match |
| Table with dataview preservation | Match |
| Section operations (find, append, replace) | Match |
| Task operations (find, list, add, setStatus, complete, move, link, unlink) | Match |
| `carryForward` returns `{ source, target }` | Match |
| `getNextTaskId` | Match |
| `TaskFilter` with status/search/hasDocLinks/hasHistory | Match |
| `MoveOpts` with under/toTop/before/after | Match |
| Edge cases table from spec | Handled (no FM, empty sections, orphan subtasks, mixed line endings, trailing newline) |
| `DataviewNode` type from diagram | Not implemented (correct decision, see M-5) |

---

### Roundtrip Safety Assessment

The dirty-flag pattern is implemented correctly:

1. Parser sets `dirty: false` on all nodes.
2. `isDocDirty()` checks preamble and all sections (including subtasks).
3. Fast path returns `doc.raw` when nothing is dirty.
4. Dirty path emits `node.raw` for clean nodes, reconstructs only dirty nodes.
5. Operations clone before mutating and mark changed nodes dirty.

One scenario to watch: if a section heading itself is modified (e.g., renamed), `section.raw` would be stale. There is no `dirty` flag on `Section` — only on nodes. Currently no operation modifies section headings, so this is not a bug, but it is a gap if that operation is added later.

---

### Verdict

The library is well-built and closely follows the spec. The important findings (I-2 and I-4) affect `carryForward` correctness and should be addressed before that function is used in production. I-1 and I-3 are cleanliness issues that reduce future confusion. Everything else is minor.

Recommended action: fix I-2 (dead code / lost status) and I-4 (label carry logic) before integrating `carryForward` into the daily note pipeline. The rest can be addressed opportunistically.

input.prompt:
Write a task document for migrating the existing Obsidian tools to use the new `src/libs/obsidian-md/` library.

## Context

The project is at `d:/PROJECTS/freddie-ai`. We just built a new Obsidian-flavored markdown parsing library at `src/libs/obsidian-md/` (OT-0013). It provides:
- `parse(markdown) → Document` — AST with typed nodes (Task, TextNode, HeadingNode, TableNode, BlankNode)
- `serialize(doc) → string` — roundtrip-safe with dirty-flag pattern
- Operations: `findSection`, `appendToSection`, `replaceSectionContent`, `findTask`, `listTasks`, `addTask`, `setTaskStatus`, `completeTask`, `moveTask`, `linkDoc`, `unlinkDoc`, `getNextTaskId`, `carryForward`
- Types: `Task` (with stable `id`, `status`, `subtasks`, `links`, `history`, `forward`, `completion`, `cancellation`, `isLabel`, `isStrikethrough`), `TaskStatus`, `TaskFilter`, `MoveOpts`, etc.

Read these files to understand what needs to migrate:

**New library (the target):**
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/types.ts`
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/operations.ts`
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/index.ts`

**Current code (what needs to change):**
- `src/libs/sections.ts` — current core section/task parser used by multiple tools
- `src/tools/obsidian/tasks.ts` — task CRUD CLI tool (add, complete, status, move, link, unlink, list, summary)
- `src/tools/obsidian/shared.ts` — constants, carry-forward, daily template helpers
- `src/tools/obsidian/sections.ts` — section-level CLI operations (read, add-decision, add-learned, etc.)
- `src/tools/obsidian/daily.ts` — daily note creation/management
- `src/tools/obsidian/summaries.ts` — weekly/monthly/yearly aggregation

**Also check for other consumers:**
- `grep -r "from.*sections" src/` and `grep -r "from.*shared" src/` to find all files importing from the old parsing code

## Migration Strategy

The goal is to replace the hand-rolled regex parsing in the existing tools with calls to the new `obsidian-md` library. Key principles:

1. **Replace internal parsing, keep CLI interfaces.** The CLI tools (`tasks.ts`, `sections.ts`, `daily.ts`, `summaries.ts`) keep their exact same CLI argument parsing and output format. Only the internal markdown parsing/manipulation changes.

2. **`src/libs/sections.ts` becomes a thin wrapper or gets eliminated.** Its functions (`findSection`, `toggleTask`, `setTaskStatus`, `appendToSection`, etc.) are now in `obsidian-md/operations.ts`. Consumers should import from `obsidian-md` directly, or `sections.ts` becomes a re-export shim during transition.

3. **`shared.ts` constants and helpers.** `SECTION_NAMES`, `TASK_STATUSES`, template generation, and carry-forward logic. The task status constants now live in `obsidian-md/types.ts`. Carry-forward is in `obsidian-md/operations.ts`. Template generation and other helpers may stay in `shared.ts` or move.

4. **`summaries.ts` gets the biggest win.** It currently duplicates parsing logic. After migration, it should use `parse()` + `listTasks()` + `findSection()` to extract data from daily/weekly notes.

Write a task document for migrating the existing Obsidian tools to use the new `src/libs/obsidian-md/` library.

## Context

The project is at `d:/PROJECTS/freddie-ai`. We just built a new Obsidian-flavored markdown parsing library at `src/libs/obsidian-md/` (OT-0013). It provides:
- `parse(markdown) → Document` — AST with typed nodes (Task, TextNode, HeadingNode, TableNode, BlankNode)
- `serialize(doc) → string` — roundtrip-safe with dirty-flag pattern
- Operations: `findSection`, `appendToSection`, `replaceSectionContent`, `findTask`, `listTasks`, `addTask`, `setTaskStatus`, `completeTask`, `moveTask`, `linkDoc`, `unlinkDoc`, `getNextTaskId`, `carryForward`
- Types: `Task` (with stable `id`, `status`, `subtasks`, `links`, `history`, `forward`, `completion`, `cancellation`, `isLabel`, `isStrikethrough`), `TaskStatus`, `TaskFilter`, `MoveOpts`, etc.

Read these files to understand what needs to migrate:

**New library (the target):**
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/types.ts`
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/operations.ts`
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/index.ts`

**Current code (what needs to change):**
- `src/libs/sections.ts` — current core section/task parser used by multiple tools
- `src/tools/obsidian/tasks.ts` — task CRUD CLI tool (add, complete, status, move, link, unlink, list, summary)
- `src/tools/obsidian/shared.ts` — constants, carry-forward, daily template helpers
- `src/tools/obsidian/sections.ts` — section-level CLI operations (read, add-decision, add-learned, etc.)
- `src/tools/obsidian/daily.ts` — daily note creation/management
- `src/tools/obsidian/summaries.ts` — weekly/monthly/yearly aggregation

**Also check for other consumers:**
- `grep -r "from.*sections" src/` and `grep -r "from.*shared" src/` to find all files importing from the old parsing code

## Migration Strategy

The goal is to replace the hand-rolled regex parsing in the existing tools with calls to the new `obsidian-md` library. Key principles:

1. **Replace internal parsing, keep CLI interfaces.** The CLI tools (`tasks.ts`, `sections.ts`, `daily.ts`, `summaries.ts`) keep their exact same CLI argument parsing and output format. Only the internal markdown parsing/manipulation changes.

2. **`src/libs/sections.ts` becomes a thin wrapper or gets eliminated.** Its functions (`findSection`, `toggleTask`, `setTaskStatus`, `appendToSection`, etc.) are now in `obsidian-md/operations.ts`. Consumers should import from `obsidian-md` directly, or `sections.ts` becomes a re-export shim during transition.

3. **`shared.ts` constants and helpers.** `SECTION_NAMES`, `TASK_STATUSES`, template generation, and carry-forward logic. The task status constants now live in `obsidian-md/types.ts`. Carry-forward is in `obsidian-md/operations.ts`. Template generation and other helpers may stay in `shared.ts` or move.

4. **`summaries.ts` gets the biggest win.** It currently duplicates parsing logic. After migration, it should use `parse()` + `listTasks()` + `findSection()` to extract data from daily/weekly notes.

5. **Output format must NOT change.** The CLI tools produce specific output that skills and agents consume. The output strings must remain identical.

## Task doc requirements

Save to `vault/notes/tasks/` following naming convention. Get current date/time and next OT number. Include:
- File-by-file migration plan showing what changes in each file
- Which functions get replaced vs kept vs deleted
- Import changes
- Acceptance criteria (existing CLI behavior must be preserved — same args, same output)
- Execution order with dependency tracking
- Risk areas (output format changes, edge cases in the switch)

## Summary

Migrate the existing Obsidian CLI tools from hand-rolled regex parsing to the new `src/libs/obsidian-md/` library. The library provides `parse()`, `serialize()`, and structured operations (findTask, listTasks, addTask, setTaskStatus, completeTask, moveTask, linkDoc, unlinkDoc, carryForward, findSection, appendToSection, etc.).

## Files to change (read all before starting)

**New library (read for API):**
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/index.ts` — re-exports
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/types.ts` — Task, Document, Section, TaskStatus, etc.
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/parser.ts` — parse()
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/serializer.ts` — serialize()
- `src/libs/obsidian-md/operations.ts` — all operations

**Files to migrate:**
- `src/libs/sections.ts` — core parser, will be replaced/deleted
- `src/tools/obsidian/tasks.ts` — task CRUD CLI
- `src/tools/obsidian/shared.ts` — constants, carry-forward, templates
- `src/tools/obsidian/sections.ts` — section CLI operations
- `src/tools/obsidian/daily.ts` — daily note management
- `src/tools/obsidian/summaries.ts` — weekly/monthly/yearly

**Also check for other importers:**
- Search for any other files importing from `libs/sections` or `tools/obsidian/shared`

## Critical constraints

1. **CLI output must not change.** The tools are called by agents/skills that parse their stdout. Same arguments, same output format.
2. **Use the new library's parse/serialize cycle.** Read file → parse() → operate on AST → serialize() → write file. The dirty-flag pattern ensures only modified lines change.
3. **Task lookup by ID, not position.** The old code matched tasks by line index. The new code uses `findTask(doc, id)` for stable ID-based lookup. For fuzzy/text-based lookups (when users pass text instead of an ID), implement an adapter that searches by content substring.
4. **Delete `src/libs/sections.ts` at the end** — after all consumers are migrated.

## Verification

After migration:

3. Verify no remaining imports of the old `libs/sections.ts`

content.text:
All green. Here's what was cleaned up:

| Item | What changed |
|------|-------------|
| **Raw string slicing eliminated** | `listTasks` and `readSection` now use `sectionBodyText(section)` from the AST — no more `content.split()` + line index math |
| **Duplicate `countItems` consolidated** | Single `countItems()` in `shared.ts`, imported by both `tasks.ts` and `daily.ts`. `buildCounts` deleted from `daily.ts` |
| **`TASK_STATUSES` simplified** | Replaced with `STATUS_NAMES` derived from `STATUS_TO_CHAR` — no more duplicated marker values |
| **`moveTask` re-parse eliminated** | `to_top` and `under` now compute the new ID upfront via `getNextTaskId()` before the move |
| **Label guard added** | `completeTask` and `setStatus` now reject group labels with clear error messages |
Let's put it through its paces. I'll run a series of operations.

  in_progress), skip labels with all-completed subtasks, capture status
  before mutating source
- Remove dead task branch from serializer
- Consolidate duplicate countItems/buildCounts into shared.ts
- Replace TASK_STATUSES map with STATUS_NAMES derived from STATUS_TO_CHAR
- Eliminate moveTask re-parse — compute IDs upfront via getNextTaskId
- Add label guards to completeTask and setStatus

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Base directory for this skill: d:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\.claude\skills\penny

Manage the Obsidian vault:

**task list**

### Sub-agent dispatch

Use the **Agent tool** with `subagent_type: "penny"` for runing the CLI coomands and parsing. Each dispatch is a bounded task — Penny returns structured results, never writes to disk.


## How to Parse

All CLI paths are relative to project root: `bun src/tools/obsidian/<module>.ts <command> [flags]`

| User says... | CLI tool + command |
|-------------|-------------------|
| "list tasks" / "show tasks" / "what's on today" | `obsidian/tasks.ts list` (default: open tasks) |
| "list all" / "show all tasks" | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --filter all` |
| "list open tasks" / "show open" | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --filter open` |
| "list done tasks" / "show completed" | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --filter done` |
| "list in-progress" / "what's in progress" | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --filter in_progress` |
| "list deferred" / "show deferred" | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --filter deferred` |
| "list urgent" | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --filter urgent` |
| 'tasks with "X"' / 'find task X' | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --search "X"` |
| 'open tasks with "X"' | `obsidian/tasks.ts list --filter open --search "X"` |
| "task summary" / "how many tasks" | `obsidian/tasks.ts summary` |
| "add task: X" | `obsidian/tasks.ts add --content "X"` |
| "add sub-task under 3: X" | `obsidian/tasks.ts add --content "X" --parent 3` |
| "mark 1.2 done" / "complete 3" | `obsidian/tasks.ts complete --task "1.2"` |
| "start task 3" / "task 3 in progress" | `obsidian/tasks.ts status --task 3 --status in_progress` |
| "defer task 5" | `obsidian/tasks.ts status --task 5 --status deferred` |
| "move task 3 to tomorrow" | `obsidian/tasks.ts move --task 3 --to-date tomorrow` |
| "nest task 3 under 1" | `obsidian/tasks.ts move --task 3 --under 1` |
| "promote 1.2" | `obsidian/tasks.ts move --task 1.2 --to-top` |
| "link doc to task 5: path/to/doc.md" | `obsidian/tasks.ts link --task 5 --doc "path/to/doc.md"` |
| "unlink doc from task 5: path/to/doc.md" | `obsidian/tasks.ts unlink --task 5 --doc "path/to/doc.md"` |
| "show decisions" / "read decisions" | `obsidian/sections.ts read --name Decisions` |
| "show learned" / "what did I learn" | `obsidian/sections.ts read --name Learned` |
| "show tomorrow" / "what's for tomorrow" | `obsidian/sections.ts read --name Tomorrow` |
| "decision: X" / "add decision: X" | `obsidian/sections.ts add-decision --content "X"` |
| "learned: X" | `obsidian/sections.ts add-learned --content "X"` |
| "tomorrow: X" | `obsidian/sections.ts add-tomorrow --content "X"` |
| "session: X" | `obsidian/sections.ts add-session --content "X"` |
| "note: X" | `obsidian/sections.ts add-note --content "X"` |
| "log: X" | `obsidian/sections.ts add-log --content "X"` |
| "meeting X at 10am with Y" | `obsidian/meeting.ts --title "X" --time "10:00 AM" --attendees "Y"` |
| "weekly summary" | `obsidian/summaries.ts weekly` |
| "monthly summary" | `obsidian/summaries.ts monthly` |
| "search for X" | `obsidian/search.ts --query "X"` |
| "show daily" / "full note" | `obsidian/daily.ts` |

## Date Handling

- Check if the request starts with a date prefix (e.g., "March 25", "tomorrow", "last Friday")
- If found, pass `--date <parsed>` to the CLI tool
- If no date, omit `--date` (defaults to today)

## Response Style

As Penny — brief, butler-style. Confirm what was done.

content.text:
Here's the overlap:

| Concern | `startup.ts` | `morning.ts` | Verdict |
|---------|-------------|-------------|---------|
| Agent souls | Loads all | — | startup only |
| Freddie knowledge | Loads all | — | startup only |
| Last session | Loads | — | startup only |
| Tasks (open/in-progress) | — | Loads via `execSync` (3 subprocess calls) | morning only |
| Memory inbox scan | — | Scans agent dirs | morning only |
| Suggestions | — | Builds from inbox + task counts | morning only |
| Greeting | — | Time-based random | morning only |

The separation is actually clean — `morning.ts` doesn't load context that `startup.ts` already handles. But the task loading is heavy — 3 `execSync` subprocess calls to `tasks.ts`. Since `morning.ts` runs under Bun, it can import directly.

input.new_string:
---
name: meeting
description: Create a meeting note linked to the daily note. Supports date, time, attendees, and agenda.
user-invocable: true
argument-hint: "[optional: date time description — e.g., '2026-03-25 10am Sprint planning with Mike and Sev']"
agent: penny
---

Create a meeting note:

**$ARGUMENTS**

### Sub-agent dispatch

Use the **Agent tool** with `subagent_type: "penny"` to run the CLI command and parse results.

### CLI

`bun src/tools/obsidian/meeting.ts --title "<title>" [--date <YYYY-MM-DD>] [--time "<HH:MM AM/PM>"] [--attendees "<names>"] [--agenda "<agenda>"]`

### How to parse arguments

Extract from `$ARGUMENTS`:
- **Date** — explicit date or default to today
- **Time** — e.g., "10am" → "10:00 AM"
- **Title** — the meeting topic (e.g., "Sprint planning")
- **Attendees** — names after "with" (e.g., "with Mike and Sev" → "Mike, Sev")
- **Agenda** — optional, any remaining detail

### Response style

As Penny — brief, butler-style. Confirm what was created and link to the note.

Much cleaner. The `shortTitle` function:
- Cuts at the first em dash (`—`) if within 60 chars — catches pattern like "Build studio index tool (tools/studio.ts) — fast search for tracks and lyrics"
- Falls back to sentence boundary (`. `)
- Hard truncates with `…` at last word boundary if still too long
- Subtasks get a tighter 50-char limit

content.content: Launching skill: bye

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: d:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\.claude\skills\bye

End the current session cleanly.

****

## What This Does

`/bye` is the session closer — the counterpart to `/morning`. Triggers reflection, syncs brain.db, consolidates memory, and logs to the daily note.

Raw context capture is handled automatically by the Stop hook (`capture-raw.ts`). No manual capture needed.

For formal dev sessions with task management, use `/dev-end` instead.

## Execution Model

**Freddie handles everything inline.** No sub-agents.

## Workflow

### Phase 1 — Reflect

Identify session metadata:
- **Agents involved** — freddie, mccall, wick, tala, etc.
- **Skill/workflow** — what was the session about
- **Summary** — one-line summary (use  if provided)
- **Outcome** — success, partial, or failed

```bash
bun src/tools/reflect.ts --agent freddie --task "{summary}" --workflow {skill} --outcome {success/partial/failed} --trace "{brief trace}"
```

### Phase 2 — Sync & Consolidate

```bash
bun src/tools/brain.ts --check
```

Check if consolidation is needed:
```bash
bun src/tools/consolidate-memory.ts --list
```

If any agent has 3+ inbox items, run:
```bash
bun src/tools/consolidate-memory.ts --auto
```

### Phase 3 — Daily Log

Log the session to today's daily note:

```bash
bun src/tools/obsidian/sections.ts add-log --content "Session: {summary}. Agents: {list}."
```

### Phase 4 — Sign Off

Print:
```
Session saved.
  Reflect: {learnings} learnings, {decisions} decisions
  Consolidation: {ran/not needed}
  Daily: logged

See you next time.
```
### Phase 1 — Reflect
